finding: showBodyRegion=hasBody&&!collapsed no longer gates on seen, so the `inline-file-preview-body` testid div renders for EVERY expanded preview (skeleton inside for off-screen ones); breaks tests/e2e/chat/mcp-resource-links-perf.spec.ts:159 which counts `inline-file-preview-body` and asserts < COUNT to prove lazy body mounting.

Resize separator aria-valuemax uses Math.round(vh*0.8) but the real clamp ceiling is maxReservedPx=max(400,0.8vh); on short viewports aria-valuemax < aria-valuenow(=400) → incoherent range flagged by axe. Use maxReservedPx()/INLINE_FILE_MIN_PX for the aria bounds.

Resize role=separator handle lacks aria-controls to the body region (body has no id); collapse chevron sets aria-expanded but no aria-controls. Inconsistent with CollapsibleBlock's aria-controls={regionId}.
Resize affordance bg-muted/60 + grip bg-border may fall short of WCAG 1.4.11 3:1 non-text contrast; verify/strengthen the handle boundary visibility.

`const { files } = Stores.MessageViewState` subscribes every InlineFilePreview to the WHOLE files map; markFileSeen fires on scroll-in, changing the map identity, so every mounted preview re-renders on every scroll-in — a scroll-driven re-render storm that undercuts the smoothness goal. Subscribe to the per-key entry instead.
Reads the whole collapsed map so toggling one message re-renders all mounted CollapsibleBlocks; a keyed selector scopes it (rare event, but genuine over-subscription).

TEST-6 runs against instant-decode data: SVGs + synchronous markdown (no async late re-measure), so it would likely pass even with the ITEM-2 fixed-height change reverted — it does not exercise the async-settle failure mode it guards.

TEST-9 (expand does not jump) passes by construction (content grows downward, row top fixed) even if useInPlaceAnchor were a no-op; it is a user-guarantee guard, not proof of the hook. The hook's logic is proven by the inPlaceAnchorDelta unit tests.
The pointer-drag resize path (setPointerCapture/pointermove/commitDrag) is untested; only keyboard ArrowDown is exercised, though the feature advertises drag resize.
commitDrag calls the store setter inside a setDragHeight functional updater (impure updater; StrictMode double-invokes it). Move the commit out of the updater.
aria-valuemin hardcodes 160 and aria-valuemax uses viewportH()*0.8, diverging from INLINE_FILE_MIN_PX / maxReservedPx(); reference the constants.

Seeds via useChatStore.setState({...} as never) inside the component rather than the SeededSurfaceEntry setup() channel; the `as never` cast hides seed-shape drift. Acceptable (matches TableDemos self-contained style) but deviates from the seeded-surface doc.

file module now imports chat-module internals (store/helpers/hook); acceptable (already a chat-extension) but contradicts an in-file comment that previously declined chat coupling — worth an explicit note.
findScrollParent getComputedStyle walk + 2-rAF runs on every keystep of a held ArrowUp/Down resize; cheap individually but unbounded per key-repeat.

Drag handle has no onPointerCancel/onLostPointerCapture; a cancelled gesture leaves dragStart.current non-null → the next hover pointermove resizes with no button pressed (stuck drag) and the height is never committed.

The 2-rAF read of topAfter assumes the virtualizer's measureElement ResizeObserver adjusted scrollOffset by then; if the size-change adjustment lands later, the residual-drift correction reads a stale topAfter and can nudge the scroller.
Rapid repeated toggles each schedule an independent capture→2rAF→scrollTop chain; overlapping chains can double-adjust since neither cancels the other.
Reviewed (correctness cluster): null-ref guards present (rowRef.current/cur, observer el check, releasePointerCapture optional-chain); no unguarded getBoundingClientRect-on-null path. Clean.

Reviewed (security cluster): window.__MSGLIST_METRICS__ is import.meta.env.DEV-gated at both the increment and the install effect (with cleanup delete), so Vite dead-code-eliminates it from production; data:/resource_link URIs are only store keys / passed to viewers, never dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Clean.
Reviewed (security cluster): no auth/permission logic touched; the new store is per-conversation client UI state only. Clean.
